description_long: This is a rectangular, weathered wooden toolbox with a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The exterior is constructed from aged wooden planks, reinforced with three horizontal wooden slats on the lid for added durability. Both short ends feature a metal handle, likely for carrying, which is inset into the wood for a flush profile. The wood is painted in a faded greenish-gray color, with significant wear and chipping that reveals the natural wood beneath. The box is assembled with visible finger joints at the corners, suggesting sturdy craftsmanship. No visible locks or latches are present, and the overall design is simple and functional, suitable for storage or transport of tools in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A weathered, rectangular wooden toolbox with metal handles and reinforced slats, painted in a faded green-gray with visible wear.
description_view_0: The front view shows the long side of the toolbox with three reinforcing slats and visible finger joints at the corners.
description_view_1: The short end view reveals a metal handle inset into the wood and the slatted construction of the lid.
description_view_2: The rear long side is similar to the front, with visible wear and finger joints but no handles or additional features.
description_view_3: The opposite short end also features an inset metal handle and the same slatted lid design.
attribute_handle_type: Rectangular, inset metal handles on each short end; fixed, flush with the wood, no ergonomic padding.
attribute_latch_mechanism: No visible latch or locking mechanism; lid appears to be held closed by gravity or friction.
attribute_compartment_configuration: No internal compartments, trays, or dividers are visible or implied; appears to be a single large compartment inside.
